title: Briny Gooey Ukrainian Sour Cream Pierogi
description: Tender dough pockets filled with a tangy, creamy sour cream and chive mixture, spherified and topped with a sprinkle of nutmeg, a festive Ukrainian dish perfect for any occasion.

ingredients.name: pierogi dough
ingredients.notes: use a gluten-free flour blend if needed

ingredients.name: chopped fresh chives
ingredients.notes: use 2 tablespoons of dried chives as a substitute

ingredients.name: granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: balances the tanginess of the sour cream

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the pierogi dough ingredients and knead until a smooth dough forms.
Divide the dough into 2 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin sheet.
Using a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass, cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the sour cream mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-circle, and press the edges together to seal the pierogi.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pierogi for 5-7 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Meanwhile, prepare the spherification mixture by dissolving 1 tablespoon of sodium alginate in 1 tablespoon of cold water.
Add the spherification mixture to the cooked pierogi and stir gently.
Using a dropper or a spoon, carefully drop small amounts of the spherified mixture into a bath of cold water.
The mixture will form into small, bite-sized balls.
Remove the balls from the water and top with a sprinkle of nutmeg.
Serve the Briny Gooey Ukrainian Sour Cream Pierogi hot, garnished with chopped fresh chives if desired.
